title: hieracium nigrescens subsp. stenolope
side_effects: Hieracium nigrescens subsp. stenolope can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. In addition, excessive consumption of the plant may lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and respiratory problems. Prolonged use of Hieracium nigrescens subsp. stenolope can also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as interact with certain medications, leading to adverse effects. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
